pi_items.source: Official NCH DreamPlan documentation (https://help.nchsoftware.com/help/en/dreamplan/win/editstory.html) and verifier.py line 102 which explicitly checks for 'A story named "Basement"'

pi_items.verified_value: Negative elevation values (e.g., -8ft, -2.5m) are required for basement stories
pi_items.source: Official NCH DreamPlan documentation (https://help.nchsoftware.com/help/en/dreamplan/win/editstory.html and https://help.nchsoftware.com/help/en/dreamplan/win/newstory.html) which states 'If the story is to be below ground level, such as a basement or garden level, enter a negative number for elevation'; verifier.py line 102 checks for 'level with negative elevation (e.g., -8ft, -2.5m)'

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a basement story in DreamPlan Home Design, starting from the built-in Contemporary House sample project. A story named 'Basement' must be created with negative elevation values (such as -8ft or -2.5m) to position it below ground level. Foundation walls are then drawn on this basement level to match the floor above. DreamPlan is real commercial software from NCH Software (https://www.nchsoftware.com) that supports multi-story home design including basements using negative elevation values.
